title: cassine laneana
intro: Cassine laneana, also known as Cassine or Kola, is a plant species native to tropical Africa. It is a medicinal plant with a long history of use in traditional medicine, valued for its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. Cassine laneana has been used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems, offering benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving circulation, and supporting immune function. The plant is commonly prepared as a decoction or infusion, and its bark, leaves, and roots are used in traditional herbal preparations to leverage its medicinal properties.
